ucs.title: [LLM] Chimera (G0114) Skeleton Key + NTDS.dit theft chain on Domain Controllers
ucs.description: Hunts the Chimera-specific tradecraft of installing a Skeleton Key implant in lsass on a DC and immediately staging NTDS.dit via ntdsutil 'IFM' or VSS shadow copies. NCC Group documented this exact pattern against Taiwanese semiconductor firms (cryptdll.dll/samsrv.dll patching followed by ntdsutil ac in ntds, ifm, create full).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.dest_category="domain_controller" OR Processes.dest="*DC*") AND ((Processes.process_name="ntdsutil.exe" AND (Processes.process="*ifm*" OR Processes.process="*create full*" OR Processes.process="*ac in ntds*")) OR (Processes.process_name="vssadmin.exe" AND Processes.process="*create shadow*" AND Processes.process="*C:*") OR (Processes.process_name="wbadmin.exe" AND Processes.process="*ntds*") OR (Processes.process_name IN ("reg.exe","reg") AND Processes.process="*save*" AND (Processes.process="*hklm\\sam*" OR Processes.process="*hklm\\security*" OR Processes.process="*hklm\\system*")) OR (Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" AND (Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll*MiniDump*" OR Processes.process="*lsass*"))) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name _time | rename Processes.* as * | stats count dc(process_name) as distinct_techniques values(process_name) as techniques values(cmdline) as cmdlines min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime by dest user | where distinct_techniques>=2 OR match(techniques,"ntdsutil") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let dcs = DeviceInfo | where DeviceType == "Server" and (OSPlatform startswith "WindowsServer") | where DeviceName has_any ("DC","PDC","ADS") or AdditionalFields has "DomainController" | distinct DeviceId, DeviceName;
let ntds_dump = DeviceProcessEvents
| where DeviceId in ((dcs | project DeviceId))
| where (FileName =~ "ntdsutil.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("ifm","create"))
   or (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "create shadow")
   or (FileName =~ "wbadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "ntds")
   or (FileName =~ "reg.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "save" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("hklm\\sam","hklm\\security","hklm\\system"))
   or (FileName =~ "rundll32.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("comsvcs.dll, MiniDump","comsvcs.dll,MiniDump"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessAccountName;
let skeleton_key = DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where DeviceId in ((dcs | project DeviceId))
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "lsass.exe"
| where FileName in~ ("cryptdll.dll","samsrv.dll") and FolderPath !startswith "C:\\Windows\\System32"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, InitiatingProcessFileName;
ntds_dump
| join kind=fullouter (skeleton_key) on DeviceName
| extend EventTime = coalesce(Timestamp, Timestamp1)
| summarize Techniques = make_set(coalesce(FileName, FileName1)), Commands = make_set(ProcessCommandLine), Loads = make_set(FolderPath) by DeviceName, bin(EventTime, 1h)
| where array_length(Techniques) >= 2 or Techniques has "ntdsutil.exe" or Loads has_any ("AppData","Temp","ProgramData")
ucs.rationale: Chimera's NCC Group/CyCraft case studies show a near-deterministic chain: drop SkeletonKeyInjector to patch cryptdll.dll/samsrv.dll inside lsass on a DC (giving them a master password to bypass MFA), then execute `ntdsutil ac in ntds, ifm, create full <path>` to grab NTDS.dit. Joining lsass image-load anomalies with ntdsutil IFM on the same DC inside an hour is highly specific to this actor's play

ucs.title: [LLM] Chimera (G0114) staged exfiltration to OneDrive/Dropbox/Google Drive via Cobalt Strike beacon servers
ucs.description: Catches Chimera's signature exfil pattern: after looting (mailboxes, IP from semiconductor R&D file shares), data is RAR'd into staging directories (often %ProgramData% or C:\Windows\Temp), then uploaded with curl/PowerShell directly to legitimate cloud storage providers. CyCraft and NCC reported Chimera using OneDrive and Google Drive as their preferred T1567.002 channels.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmds values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("rar.exe","winrar.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe","curl.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") AND (Processes.process="*-hp*" OR Processes.process="*-p*" OR Processes.process="*a -r*" OR Processes.process="*onedrive.live.com*" OR Processes.process="*api.onedrive.com*" OR Processes.process="*graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me/drive*" OR Processes.process="*dropboxapi.com*" OR Processes.process="*content.dropboxapi.com*" OR Processes.process="*googleapis.com/upload/drive*" OR Processes.process="*Invoke-WebRequest*" OR Processes.process="*Invoke-RestMethod*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name _time | rename Processes.* as * | eval is_archive=if(match(process_name,"(?i)(rar|7z)"),1,0), is_upload=if(match(cmds,"(?i)(onedrive|dropbox|googleapis|graph\.microsoft).*"),1,0) | stats sum(is_archive) as archive_events sum(is_upload) as upload_events values(cmds) as cmds values(process_name) as tools min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime by dest user | where archive_events>=1 AND upload_events>=1 AND (lastTime-firstTime) < 7200 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | join type=left dest [| tstats summariesonly=t sum(All_Traffic.bytes_out) as bytes_out from datamodel=Network_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_category="cloud_storage" OR All_Traffic.dest IN ("*.onedrive.com","*.dropbox.com","*.googleapis.com") by All_Traffic.src | rename All_Traffic.src as dest] | where bytes_out > 50000000
ucs.kql:
let archive_stage = DeviceProcessEvents
| where FileName in~ ("rar.exe","winrar.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-hp"," a -r"," -p","-v")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("\\ProgramData\\","\\Windows\\Temp\\","\\AppData\\","C:\\Users\\Public\\")
| project StageTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, ArchiveCmd=ProcessCommandLine, ArchiveTool=FileName;
let cloud_upload = DeviceProcessEvents
| where FileName in~ ("curl.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","bitsadmin.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (
    "onedrive.live.com","api.onedrive.com","graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me/drive",
    "content.dropboxapi.com","dropboxapi.com",
    "www.googleapis.com/upload/drive","oauth2.googleapis.com")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("PUT","upload","Invoke-WebRequest","Invoke-RestMethod","-T ","--upload-file")
| project UploadTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, UploadCmd=ProcessCommandLine, UploadTool=FileName;
archive_stage
| join kind=inner (cloud_upload) on DeviceId
| where UploadTime between (StageTime .. (StageTime + 2h))
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where RemoteUrl has_any ("onedrive","dropbox","googleapis") or RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | summarize BytesOut=sum(toint(coalesce(tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).bytes_out),"0"))) by DeviceId, bin(Timestamp,1h)
) on DeviceId
| extend MinutesBetween = datetime_diff('minute', UploadTime, StageTime)
| project StageTime, UploadTime, MinutesBetween, DeviceName, AccountName, ArchiveTool, ArchiveCmd, UploadTool, UploadCmd, BytesOut
| order by StageTime desc
ucs.rationale: Generic cloud-upload rules drown in noise, but Chimera's documented chain is RAR-with-password into a staging dir on a server (not endpoint), followed within ~2h by curl/PowerShell PUT to OneDrive/Dropbox/Google Drive APIs from the SAME host. Joining the archive event to the upload event on the same DeviceId+narrow time window suppresses developer/backup FPs and isolates the semiconductor-IP/airli
